title: International waterways must remain safe and unimpeded: S. Jaishankar
content: Attacks on seafarers, civilian shipping or infrastructure cannot be countenanced, says External Affairs Minister at East Asia Summit; Ministry hits back at Pakistan Deputy PM’s comments on Kashmir and Indus Water Treaty

title: Flood toll climbs to 47 in Assam; over 7 lakh affected
content: Chief Minister Himanta Biswa Sarma says localised heavy rainfall 436% above normal and cloudbursts upstream in Nagaland combined to cause the unprecedented devastation

title: BJP leaders, allies urge Centre to treat youth protests with empathy
content: Former HRD Minister Murli Manohar Joshi says the protest should not be treated as a law-and-order problem; sections of the BJP and its allies in the NDA feel the issue has gone beyond NEET and education

title: Sonam Wangchuk has broken his fast, says Leh Apex Body co-convenor
content: He broke the fast in the presence of two Union Ministers on certain conditions, he says;  Govt gave a written assurance that cases against students will be taken back, if they are not violent, Mr. Dorjay says

title: Protests over paper leak intensify in States
content: Student outfits, civil society groups, and political parties hit the streets demanding resignation of Union Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan; protest marches turn confrontational in several districts of Kerala; police stop protesters in A.P.; security personnel resort to baton-charge in Bihar

title: Jantar Mantar protest LIVE: Wangchuk breaks 26-day fast; relieved, says CJP founder Dipke, but vows to continue protest till Pradhan resigns
content: He broke the fast in the presence of two Union Ministers on certain conditions, he says; the government gave a written assurance that cases against students will be taken back if they are not violent, Mr. Dorjay says; the government also gave a written assurance that they will consider compensation for NEET aspirants who took their lives, he adds

title: Three days since July 20 violence, Delhi Police, MHA yet to acknowledge police excesses
content: Eenesh, a resident of Chhattisgarh, who took part in the protest, refuted the police claim that protesters threw bricks at them

title: CPI criticises Kerala Chief Minister on Waqf issue
content: The reconstitution of the Board by including two non-Muslims had grown into a conflict issue between the Kerala Waqf Board and the State government

title: PM reaches out to students; Sonam Wangchuk ends fast
content: Activist ends his hunger strike in the presence of two Ministers; Modi, in a late-night social media post, says Cabinet will discuss paper leak issue today; Centre transfers Higher Education Secretary

title: Governor emphasises need for coordination of tradition and technology
content: Governor of Karnataka Thawar Chand Gehlot has emphasised the need for coordinating heritage with modern technology so as to introduce and popularise Ayurveda at the global level

title: PM Modi promises 'more strict actions against paper leaks' amid CJP protests
content: In a self-recorded video shared on X, the Prime Minister announced that the Union Cabinet will take up a draft Bill providing for Fast-Track Courts and stringent punishment for offenders. he said, “I have issued directions to departments to set up Fast-Track Courts

title: Hundreds gather in Kerala’s Kochi to express solidarity with Delhi student protest
content: Participants express solidarity with the ongoing student protests in New Delhi over the alleged NEET question paper leak and condemn police action against the protesters

title: Aadhaar verification for Tatkal bookings not violative of privacy rights: Kerala High Court
content: Court notes that the requirement for authentication for Tatkal tickets was justified based on experiences of alleged deceit, manipulation and bulk booking, which denied access to the ticketing system for genuine users

title: Vijay’s Jana Nayagan hits the screens across State; cinemas see good footfall
content: Cinema owners in the southern districts report a noticeable increase in women turning up for ‘first day’ shows. For Friday, most cinemas in Chennai say occupancy levels are around 70%-75%

title: World Hepatitis Day webinar to be held on July 27
content: Experts will discuss causes, transmission, prevention, early detection and advances in treatment, while addressing common misconceptions about viral hepatitis

title: A namesake creates trouble for former Kerala Minister P.K. Sreemathi
content: CPI(M) leader accused of drawing a pension claiming to be the wife of a former MLA. Allegation stems from confusion with another P.K. Sreemathi, widow of former Kunnamkulam MLA P.R. Krishnan, who received the pension till 2020

title: Bengaluru rallies behind Delhi student protest, demand resignation of Dharmendra Pradhan
content: Raising slogans, the protestors lit candles and switched on their mobile torches as night descended, in an expression of solidarity. They held placards with clever puns and word play

title: Farmers oppose acquisition of land for HAL expansion at Gubbi in Tumakuru district
content: Farmers are worried that HAL’s long-term plans to expand the facility by adding infrastructure such as a hospital and other amenities for its employees could lead to further acquisition of agricultural land

title: Set KPSC in order before protesting over NEET, BJP tells Karnataka Congress
content: The Opposition BJP alleges that the government here has failed to fix responsibility for the death of 11 youth in the stampede near Chinnaswamy Stadium and for the lathi-charge on unemployed youth in North Karnataka who were protesting the delay in recruitment for government jobs

title: No evidence against Sanjeev Mukhiya in NEET-UG 2024 case: CBI
content: Bihar police had named him in the FIR as he was found involved in certain other examination paper theft/leak cases, the agency said

title: Muttil tree-felling case: Kerala High Court upholds cheating charges against Augustine brothers
content: The court found merit in allegations by M.M. Aliyar, who claimed he was misled into believing the timber came with proper permits, a claim the brothers disputed.

title: Kerala High Court quashes sexual harassment case against Boby Chemmanur
content: Court granted Chemmanur’s plea to quash the proceedings after the actor expressed her willingness to resolve the matter amicably, citing his public apology.

title: Kerala government reconstitutes prison advisory committees
content: The panels will be chaired by the Director General of Prisons; it will also include non-official members nominated by the government

title: Delhi High Court notifies designated court to deal with criminal cases arising from exam paper leaks
content: Delhi High Court designates Judge Anu Grover Baliga as Special Judge of the fast-track court to deal with criminal cases arising out of paper leaks and the use of unfair means in public examinations

title: Govt. to sell quality rice at subsidised prices through rythu bazaars from August 1
content: Steady increase in rice prices prompts government to intervene, says Civil Supplies Minister Nadendla Manohar

title: 555 cases pending against sitting and former MPs, MLAs in Kerala
content: Court observes that data provided by the High Court administration is in variance with the report by the State Police Chief, which cited 256 pending cases. It directs SPC to correct the statistics

title: The National Democratic Alliance’s increased strength in the Lok Sabha since 2024 | Graphics
content: As the monsoon session began this week, the Trinamool Congress and Shiv Sena (UBT) rebel factions have added 26 more seats to the NDA camp to 318 MPs

title: Police should better equip themselves to deal with changing nature of crimes: CM
content: The tendency of some anti-social elements to create unrest in society by making abusive and inflammatory comments on social media platforms has to be curbed, says Chandrababu Naidu

title: At least 1,200 rescued as torrential rain pounds south Gujarat; PM Modi, Amit Shah review situation
content: One rain-related death reported in Tapi district; IMD issues red alert for Valsad, Navsari, Dang, Surat and Tapi districts for Thursday and Friday; NDRF, SDRF and IAF placed on alert

title: Women’s reservation should be implemented without delimitation: Justice Hariparanthaman
content: If the increase in Lok Sabha seats is based on population, southern States will stand to lose adequate representation in the Lower House, says former Madras High Court judge

title: Delhi Police’s preventive detention power under NSA extended till October 18
content: Statement says the order is a routine extension/renewal of powers under the NSA, which is issued every three months

title: Kerala University Syndicate scraps ₹2-crore sculpture park project
content: The sculpture park, proposed in the university’s 2025-26 budget by the previous Left-dominant Syndicate, was expected to feature statues of eminent personalities including Karl Marx, Charles Darwin, Socrates, and Rabindranath Tagore

title: Bangladesh PM Tarique Rahman invited to BRICS summit in Delhi
content: Bangladesh’s Minister for Foreign Affairs says the Indian government has invited Mr. Rahman to participate in the summit as a guest

title: Over 6.17 lakh Waqf properties approved on UMEED portal
content: The portal is part of government’s ongoing initiative to digitise and streamline Waqf property records

title: No debate without Pradhan’s resignation, Opposition tells govt. as it hits the streets
content: INDIA bloc closes ranks on paper leaks, rejects discussion offer and carries protest from Parliament to Gandhi Smriti in a show of solidarity with agitating students; in the meeting, it was felt that a debate would offer the govt. a chance to grandstand its way through by leaning on its numerical strength in both Houses of Parliament

title: Pawan calls for enhanced surveillance to combat red sanders smuggling
content: Addressing a review meeting, the Deputy Chief Minister directs officials to dismantle the smuggling networks down to their roots through a zero-tolerance approach

title: 15 of 39 posts in National Testing Agency lie vacant, says Minister of State for Education
content: In a written reply to CPI(M) Rajya Sabha member V. Sivadasan, the Minister says 24 employees who are employed with the organisation have all been appointed on deputation from various institutions

title: ‘Jana Nayagan’ movie review: ‘Thalapathy’ Vijay bids adieu to cinema with an overstuffed crowd-pleaser
content: Vijay’s farewell film is in equal parts a crowd-pleasing tribute and a political manifesto, wrapped within an uneven but engaging masala entertainer

title: Students' disappointment understandable: Tendulkar issues statement on NEET agitation
content: Mr. Tendulkar said he is hopeful of a solution coming through and stressed on the need to promote a culture which rewards honesty and in which merit wins

title: Sonam Wangchuk ends hunger strike; PM Modi vows stricter actions against paper leaks
content: Sonam Wangchuk ended his 26-day hunger strike after government assurances on student demands. Prime Minister Modi announced a new bill to combat examination paper leaks. This proposed legislation includes fast-track courts and tougher punishments for offenders. The Union Cabinet will discuss the bill on Friday before Parliament next week. The government aims to protect students' interests and make the examination system foolproof.

title: 2,500 detained, homes of 2 LeT terrorists razed in Kashmir after cop killing
content: Security forces demolished two Lashkar-e-Taiba terrorist homes overnight in Kashmir. Over two thousand five hundred people were detained for questioning following a police killing. The operation aimed to dismantle terrorist support networks across the region. Officials reviewed security arrangements for the ongoing Amarnath Yatra pilgrimage. Political leaders criticized the demolitions and mass detentions as counter-productive.

title: Astronauts may grow Medicines: UC San Diego tests space pharma
content: More than half the medicines stocked on the ISS expire within three years, a serious problem for a Mars mission lasting around 400 days. A new University of California San Diego study offers a fix: grow the drugs on board. Researchers cultivated cowpea mosaic virus, a cancer-fighting plant virus, on tobacco-relative and black-eyed pea plants in simulated microgravity, then extracted it without killing the plant.

title: NIA arrests absconding PFI district chief in Kerala's Sreenivasan murder case
content: The National Investigation Agency arrested a key absconder in the Sreenivasan murder case. Muhammadali K.P, a PFI Palakkad district president, was apprehended upon arriving from Saudi Arabia. He was a prominent perpetrator and harbored assailants involved in the brutal killing of Sreenivasan. The PFI conspiracy aimed to establish Islamic Rule in India by 2047. Investigations revealed PFI imparted arms training and collected target information through its various wings.

title: Canada college to protesting Indian students: Seek legal advice, retain imp documents
content: Indian students protest after Portage College's business management diploma graduates faced work visa rejections.  The Immigration, Refugees and Citizenship Canada stated their programs were not eligible for permits.  Portage College encourages affected students to retain important documents and seek legal advice.  The college cannot overturn decisions made by the immigration authority.  Affected students should consult official government materials and obtain independent advice.

title: Taylor Swift, Travis Kelce reportedly have bigger plans for wedding photos
content: Taylor Swift has reportedly delayed releasing her wedding photos with Travis Kelce because she is waiting for the "perfect moment." Sources claim the singer wants the images to be part of a larger storytelling project, possibly a documentary or coffee-table book, rather than a magazine exclusive. Insiders say Swift prefers to control her narrative, creating meaningful cultural moments instead of chasing publicity or financial gain.

title: Ships trapped near Strait of Hormuz could become nurseries for invasive species
content: Ships lingering in the Persian Gulf could unintentionally facilitate the spread of harmful marine invaders worldwide. Extended anchorage allows marine organisms to thrive on the hulls, creating significant biofouling concerns that threaten global maritime commerce. These invasive species can inflict extensive ecological and economic harm on coastal areas. It is imperative for nations to join forces to address biofouling issues and prevent future outbreaks of invasion.

title: Florida’s python hunt used 3m camera photos and found only four snakes
content: In Florida, scientists discovered that captive female pythons failed to attract wild males. Utilizing artificial intelligence, researchers sifted through millions of camera images, revealing few python sightings. Additionally, attempts to use mammalian scent lures for detecting the invasive Burmese pythons proved futile. However, there is optimism as removal strategies in the Everglades are progressing, bolstered by hunting and radio tracking initiatives aimed at controlling the invasive species population.

title: Amazon cuts jobs in team focused on tech that Google, Microsoft says is their ‘biggest’ goals
content: Amazon has reduced roles within its Artificial General Intelligence unit.  This restructuring impacts teams focused on model customization and data services.  The company continues significant investment in artificial intelligence development and infrastructure.  These job cuts follow recent leadership changes within Amazon's AI division.  Amazon aims to sharpen its focus on key customer-centric AI initiatives.

title: 'Talk without condition such as resignation': Govt to oppn leaders over NEET debate
content: The government is engaging opposition leaders to resolve the Parliament deadlock over the NEET paper leak.  Discussions aim to end disruptions which have stalled proceedings since the Monsoon session began.  The Centre is ready for debate without preconditions, including minister's resignation.  A meeting of floor leaders is likely soon to build consensus for the discussion.  This outreach follows repeated adjournments due to protests on examination irregularities.

title: Blake Lively suffers fresh setback after Swift-Kelce wedding snub
content: Taylor Swift and Travis kelce never invited Blake Lively to their $20 million wedding and it might have caused a big new issue for the popular Hollywood actress.

title: Marc Cucurella's untied hair hides an emotional promise to his autistic son
content: Marc Cucurella's long hair carries a deeply personal meaning. The Spain defender reportedly keeps it untied so his autistic son, Mateo, can easily recognize him on the pitch. Cucurella and partner Claudia Rodriguez have also shared their emotional journey after Mateo's autism diagnosis and how it transformed their family's life.

title: Giannis Antetokounmpo's remarks about wife spark online controversy
content: Giannis Antetokounmpo's wife, Mariah, has ben often targetted by trolls for her looks. As Giannis Antetokounmpo defended her a few hours ago, things have now taken a new turn.

title: Horoscope today: Daily astrological predictions for July 24
content: Horoscope Today: Read daily horoscope predictions for July 24, 2026. Know all about the astrological events and influences that will be affecting each of the 12 zodiac signs. Our astrologer has analyzed the movements of the planets and the alignment of the stars to bring you the most accurate and up-to-date horoscope predictions for the day ahead. Get ready to discover what the stars have in store for you today.

title: '400 Pak handles identified': Delhi Police reveals Op Sindoor connection in NEET protests
content: Delhi Police identified and blocked over 400 Pakistani social media handles. These accounts are spreading misinformation and rumors amid ongoing protests. The same handles were active during Operation Sindoor and aim to provoke people. Police urged citizens, especially students, not to fall for fake content. Delhi Police respects citizens' right to peaceful protest and fact-checking.

title: 'For India's future': Gill urges compassion, mutual respect amid student protests
content: Shubman Gill urged compassion and respect for students protesting exam paper leaks. He believes India's youth deserve opportunities to learn and shape the nation's future. Several prominent Indian athletes have also voiced their support for the protesting students. They emphasise the importance of honesty and merit in the education system. The athletes hope for a fair resolution that protects students' hard work and aspirations.

title: 17 metro stations in Delhi to remain closed tomorrow starting 7.30am; check list
content: The DMRC has closed 17 metro stations in central Delhi from 7.30am Friday until further notice due to security concerns amid ongoing CJP protests at Jantar Mantar. While interchange facilities remain operational at key stations, commuters have been advised to plan alternate routes as heightened security and restrictions continue in the area.

title: Lamine Yamal, Inés García spotted shopping amid relationship buzz
content: Lamine Yamal and his girlfriend Inés García Santos have dominated headlines since the last few days as many have raised doubts on their roamnce. Amid this, a rumor about them shopping at luxury stores have sparked chaos.

title: NEET row: Delhi HC notifies special fast-track court, judge following PM's announcement
content: The Delhi High Court has designated a special fast-track court at Rouse Avenue to exclusively hear cases related to paper leaks and unfair practices under the Public Examinations Act, 2024. The move follows Prime Minister Narendra Modi's announcement of fast-track courts amid ongoing protests over the alleged NEET paper leak.

title: 'It's a dream': Emotional Ashok Sharma dedicates India debut to elder brother
content: Fast bowler Ashok Sharma made his international debut for India against Zimbabwe. He dedicated this significant career milestone to his elder brother's sacrifice. Sharma's journey highlights perseverance and family support in pursuing cricket dreams. His rapid rise followed strong domestic performances and IPL success. The young pacer received his maiden India cap from bowling coach Sunil Joshi.

title: DH Lawrence’s quote on relationships: A thought-provoking reflection on love and inner conflict
content: D. H. Lawrence challenged literary conventions with his frank explorations of human emotion. His novels, once scandalous, now inspire scholars and readers with their depth. Lawrence believed industrialization alienated people from genuine feelings and connections. His writing style focused on internal emotional lives and nature symbolism. Today, his works are studied for their insights into human nature and freedom.

title: Support staff row leaves CWG-bound Lisha Das in 'immense emotional distress'
content: Lisha has sent the email to officials of the Indian Olympic Association (IOA), Cycling Federation of India (CFI), Paralympic Committee of India (PCI) and the Sports Ministry.

title: DDA launches housing scheme with 25% discount: 1,200+ flats, starting from Rs 33 lakh
content: The Delhi Development Authority has launched a new housing scheme for government employees and professionals. This scheme offers over twelve hundred ready-to-move flats with a significant twenty-five percent discount. Registration for these affordable homes opens on July twenty-fourth and booking begins August fifteenth. Flats are located in Narela and will be allotted on a first come, first served basis.

title: 'He is fearless': Shreyas hails Sooryavanshi after record-breaking blitz vs Zimbabwe
content: Teenage prodigy Vaibhav Sooryavanshi smashed a record fifty off nineteen balls. He became the youngest player to achieve this international milestone. India secured a seven-wicket victory in the opening T20I match. Captain Shreyas Iyer celebrated his first win as T20I skipper. The bowlers restricted Zimbabwe to 125 for seven runs.

title: 'Didn't want to live apart': 3 sisters marry their social media cameraman in UP's Amroha
content: Two sisters and their cousin married the same man, their social media cameraman. They chose this unusual union to avoid living separately after marriage. The sisters explained their decision stemmed from a desire to remain together always. The cameraman agreed to the marriage respecting their deep emotional bond. Legal experts state this marriage holds no recognition under Hindu law.

title: 'Failure is okay, cheating is not': Sachin Tendulkar speaks out on NEET protests
content: Sachin Tendulkar urged society to uphold hard work and meritocracy amid NEET exam protests. He stressed that adults must shape a culture valuing effort over shortcuts. Students' disappointment over unrewarded hard work is understandable and should be addressed. Other cricketers like Shikhar Dhawan and Mohammad Kaif also voiced concerns. They called for fair resolutions and an end to student mistreatment during protests.

title: Tyreek Hill posts powerful workout update after grim medical prediction
content: In an encouraging update, Tyreek Hill shared his journey of recovery following a significant leg injury that involved two surgeries. Doctors had reservations about his future walking ability, but Hill's latest video highlights his impressive progress and optimistic mindset towards recovery and strength. He reportedly intends to announce his comeback plans at a time he  deems appropriate.

title: Premium petrol to remain ethanol-free; no plan to blend beyond 20%: Govt to Parliament
content: Premium petrol grades will continue to be ethanol-free for consumers.  The government has no plans to reintroduce lower ethanol blends like E0 or E10.  Current ethanol blending in regular petrol will remain at twenty percent.  No widespread adverse impacts have been reported from E20 fuel usage.  Future decisions on higher blending will follow extensive studies and consultations.

title: IndiGo offers crisis management plan to settle CCI probe into flight disruption case
content: IndiGo has proposed commitments to the Competition Commission of India. These proposals aim to address concerns over past flight disruptions and unfair practices. The airline will establish a crisis management group and a passenger grievance mechanism. IndiGo also offered to temporarily hand over airport slots during major disruptions. The CCI is now seeking public comments on these proposed commitments.

title: CWG 2026: Putul Sonowal stuns world champion Ryan Bester in lawn bowls thriller
content: Putul Sonowal achieved a sensational upset against the reigning world champion in lawn bowls.  Rupa Rani Tirkey and Pinki Singh also survived a thriller in the Women's Pairs event.  Sonowal showed remarkable composure to defeat a decorated Canadian opponent in his debut.  The Indian women's pair secured victory after a tense tie-break against Malta.  These wins have raised hopes for India's lawn bowls campaign at the games.

title: Trump adds Israel strings to Saudi nuclear deal, throwing pact into uncertain terrain
content: US President Trump linked the Saudi nuclear deal to Abraham Accords normalization.  This condition was not previously stated when the agreement was unveiled.  Saudi Arabia maintains its stance on Palestinian statehood progress.  The deal faces scrutiny in Congress and among critics.  This move complicates diplomatic efforts and future negotiations.

title: ‘Must be handled with empathy’: BJP veteran MM Joshi breaks ranks, backs NEET protesters
content: Veteran BJP leader Murli Manohar Joshi supported students protesting the alleged NEET paper leak. He criticized police action against demonstrators as merciless and urged empathy. The government stated its readiness for a debate on the examination system. Opposition parties demanded the Education Minister's resignation and a parliamentary discussion. Prime Minister Modi announced fast-track courts for paper leak offenders.

title: Salman Rushdie Testifies At Knife Attacker's US Terror Trial
content: Novelist Salman Rushdie on Thursday testified at the federal trial of the man who tried to kill him with a knife at a New York cultural center in 2022.

title: "Long Negotiations": Sonam Wangchuk Ends Hunger Strike After 26 Days
content: Sonam Wangchuk said his decision to end his fast followed a long negotiation on various conditions and in view of possible violence in the country.

title: US Imposes New Tariffs On 60 Countries Over Forced Labour Concerns
content: The United States said Thursday that it would impose new tariffs on 60 trading partners over forced labor concerns, replacing an expiring global duty rolled out by President Donald Trump earlier this year.

title: Trump Says Xi Jinping Will Visit US On September 24
content: US President Donald Trump said on Thursday that he will discuss artificial intelligence with Chinese President Xi Jinping when he visits the United States on September 24.

title: CJP Protest LIVE Updates: CJP, Union Ministers May Meet At 12.30 PM Today: Sources
content: CJP Jantar Mantar protest LIVE: The Cockroach Janta Party (CJP) protesters are continuing their sit-in at Jantar Mantar.

title: Trump Says Iran Has "Some Evil Intentions", Cannot Be Allowed To Have Nukes
content: US President Donald Trump on Thursday reiterated his stance that Tehran cannot be allowed to have nuclear weapons and that Washington is doing "extremely well" as the conflict continues between the two adversaries.

title: Assam Floods: 6 More Deaths In 24 Hours, Over 7 Lakh People Affected
content: Assam Floods: The Assam flood toll rose to 47 with six more deaths reported on Thursday, while the number of people affected by the deluge increased to over 7.21 lakh in 11 districts, an official bulletin said.

title: "Worried, Waiting": Sonam Wangchuk's Brother To NDTV Before He Ended Fast
content: Wangchuk joined the satirical Cockroach Janata Party (CJP)-led protest over the NEET paper leak at Delhi's Jantar Mantar last month, demanding the resignation of Union Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan.

title: PM Promises "More Strict Actions" Against Leaks Today As Protests Continue
content: "Paper leak is not an ordinary issue. It is very painful for lakhs of students and their families. That is why many steps have been taken in the last two and a half months," PM Narendra Modi said

title: Trump Reports Over $1.4 Billion In Income From Crypto Ventures
content: The filings, his annual disclosure for 2025 with the US Office of Government Ethics, disclosed that his companies received almost $800 million from World Liberty Financial, a crypto venture he and his sons co-founded.

title: Parliament Session 2026 Highlights: Opposition MPs Protest At Parliament Over NEET Leak, Demand Dharmendra Pradhan's Resignation
content: Parliament Monsoon Session 2026 Highlights: The proceedings in both Houses have been stalled because of government-opposition deadlock as the former insists on readiness for debate and the latter remains bent on the Education Minister's resignation before initiating debate.

title: Pak-Based Handles Active During Op Sindoor Spread Rumours Amid Protests: Cops
content: In a post on social media, a Delhi Police official said Pakistan-based social media handles were taking advantage of the prevailing situation to circulate rumours, fake posts and edited videos with the intent of instigating people, particularly students.

title: BJP's Bengal Election Strategy To Be Taught As Case Study In IIM Calcutta
content: The case will be made available to management institutes across the world through the Case Research Centre of IIM Calcutta so that any institution that wants to teach the case can do so.

title: Vineet Joshi, Removed As Education Secretary, Was Chief Of CBSE, Testing Body NTA
content: Joshi holds a B.Tech in Mechanical Engineering from IIT Kanpur and an MBA from the Indian Institute of Foreign Trade (IIFT), New Delhi.

title: Education Secretary Vineet Joshi Replaced Amid CJP Protest Over Paper Leaks
content: Naresh Pal Gangwar has been appointed as new Education Secretary after Joshi was moved to the Panchayati Raj ministry.

title: Video: Passengers Climb On Roof Of Sinking Bus In Flood-Hit Gujarat, Rescued
content: Nanapodha Police reached the spot after receiving information. Cops, along with locals, launched the rescue operation to save the passengers.

title: "Not Enough Pain": Report Says Trump May Decide On "Massive" Iran Attack Soon
content: Speaking to Axios on Thursday, Trump said he has not yet made a final decision on it but that such an attack would have consequences.

title: Weather Highlights: Rain In Delhi-NCR Brings Down Temperature
content: Weather Today, Delhi-NCR Rain, Monsoon Live Updates: Delhi NCR received rain lowering temperatures to 33.6C with a yellow alert issued.

title: 17 Delhi Metro Stations To Remain Shut From 7.30 AM Today
content: The Delhi Metro Rail Corporation (DMRC) said late on Thursday that the stations will remain closed from 7.30 am on Friday until further instructions, citing security reasons.

title: Delhi University Issues "Stay Away From Jantar Mantar" Advisory To Students
content: The DU administration also flagged a surge in fake news and urged students to exercise extreme caution before sharing or reacting to information.

title: Won’t forget India’s rescue of 12 Chinese crew members : China's defence attache
content: china's newly appointed Defence Attache to India, Rear Admiral You Jian, thanked the Indian military for rescuing 12 Chinese crew members last year . you said last month that the rescue operation was aimed at improving relations between the two countries .
